name: Divide and Conquer Heuristic Registry
summary: Family-local interpretation of shared heuristics for split, recurse, and merge algorithms.

family_notes: Uneven partitions create depth pressure and reduce parallel usefulness.
A split should remain balanced enough that one branch does not dominate the search.
admissibility_notes: Imbalanced splits should be surfaced before recursion commits to them.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when one partition repeatedly dominates the decomposition.
uncertainty_notes: Best treated as a split-quality warning rather than a hard failure.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared split heuristic.

family_notes: Depth can grow too quickly for the input scale or split plan.
A recursion pattern that gets too deep should be rebalanced or replaced.
admissibility_notes: Depth growth should be monitored before it becomes the primary bottleneck.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when recursion depth grows faster than expected for the input size.
uncertainty_notes: Use when recursion depth is itself the bottleneck.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared recursion heuristic.

family_notes: Merging should not dominate total work once subproblems are solved.
A combine step that dominates runtime should be monitored or revised.
admissibility_notes: Combine-stage bottlenecks should be validated before further tuning.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when combine work dominates overall runtime or memory growth.
uncertainty_notes: Use as a runtime-shaping heuristic when combine cost threatens scalability.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared merge-cost heuristic.

family_notes: Subproblems should cover the full input without gaps or overlap.
Fragmented coverage usually means the decomposition plan needs validation.
admissibility_notes: Coverage gaps or overlaps should be treated as structural defects.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when decomposition leaves missing or duplicated coverage.
uncertainty_notes: Useful wherever a decomposition can leave missing or duplicated coverage.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared coverage heuristic.

family_notes: Partitioning assumptions should stay consistent across recursive steps.
If the split plan violates declared constraints, reject it early.
admissibility_notes: Decomposition invariants should be checked before further recursion.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when split outputs violate declared family constraints.
uncertainty_notes: Useful as a family-local admissibility signal.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of a shared admissibility heuristic.

audit.rationale: Demonstrates that a non-signal family can interpret the shared heuristic surface without redefining it.
audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the generic warning signs that matter most for split-and-merge algorithms and the structural responses they justify.
uncertainty_notes: The registry intentionally interprets shared heuristics locally without redefining the canonical identifiers.

references.title: Divide and conquer heuristic registry
references.note: Local transitional family registry for shared heuristic interpretation.
